extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won after VAN POPPEL Danny delivered a perfectly timed leadout, exploiting KRISTOFF's inability to come around despite positioning in the slipstream.
tactical_patterns: ZIMMERMAN Gael attacked mid-race attempting to fracture peloton before finale, suggesting early aggression to shed sprinters' leadout trains.
BORA positioned KRISTOFF in GERMAINE Biniam's wheel after Wright's leadout, revealing their reluctance to commit VAN POPPEL early.
REINDEERS Jens participated in both breakaways then regrouped post-Mammalstein, exploiting double-break tactic to exhaust peloton control teams.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: return to top form after difficult season, suggests previous struggles were form not ability
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: executed leadout so dominant he could sit up celebrating, elevating BORA's sprint train credibility
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: neutral: positioned perfectly in GERMAINE's wheel but couldn't convert, indicating slight power decline despite tactical acumen
rider_signals.GERMAINE Biniam: negative: led out too early on city circuit, burned matches without support infrastructure for technical finish
rider_signals.REINDEERS Jens: positive: survived two breakaways including regrouping after dropped, signals exceptional diesel engine for future escape tactics
